free_text: Level Structure in 15O from the Proton Bombardment
ORNL 5.5 MV Van de Graaff accelerator
A differential pumped gaseous target scattering
chamber containing 99.98 % pure natural nitrogen gas
Surface barrier detectors placed at selected
The incident beam energy was determined by calibrating
the analyzing magnet with the known resonance at
section measurements resulted in an estimated normal
distribution uncertainty with a standard deviation
of 4 % exclusive of counting statistics. Energy
losses of protons passing through the chamber gas
were determined by observing energy shifts of the
12C(p,el) resonance as a function of chamber pressure.
The uncertainty in this determination was about
3.3 keV for typical operating conditions. Accuracy
